metadata.description: Community-maintained MLB National League injury database

injuries.details: Tore left ACL near end of May 2024 — his second major knee injury (tore right ACL in July 2021). Currently taking regular batting practice and facing live pitching. Has NOT been cleared to do cutting drills. The Braves are being cautious to avoid lingering discomfort that plagued him throughout the 2022 season after his first ACL return. No definitive return date set; will need a Minor League rehab stint once cleared for cutting and outfield play. Manager Brian Snitker: 'When he comes back, we want him to be able to go. We're going to make sure he is ready to go when we do turn him loose.'
injuries.previous_injury_note: Previously tore right ACL on July 10, 2021; returned late April 2022 but dealt with lingering discomfort all season.

sources.title: Braves cautious with Ronald Acuña Jr.'s rehab
Ronald Acuña Jr. on knee injury recovery, 2025 expectations

players.name: Mookie Betts

injuries.details: An undiagnosed stomach illness caused Betts to lose approximately 20 pounds in two weeks during spring 2025. He missed the season-opening Tokyo Series. He described feeling like his body was 'eating itself.' Returned at ~157 lbs instead of his customary ~175 lbs. After a strong first 15 games (.304/.400/.554), he struggled to a .637 OPS over his next 91 games. By end of July 2025, his OPS was .682 — the worst stretch of his career. Has since improved, hitting .330/.405/.454 since Aug 5, 2025. Now back to his customary playing weight and adjusting to a supporting-cast mindset.

Removed in the first inning of a game in Washington due to lower back pain. Walked in the top of the first and scored on Freeman's double, but was replaced by Miguel Rojas at shortstop when the Dodgers took the field. Status update expected after the game.

players.personal_note: Also dealt with the death of his stepfather during the 2025 season, compounding the physical and mental toll of the illness. Betts described going through phases of anger, sadness, and eventual acceptance.

sources.title: Mookie Betts returns from illness and struggles
Mookie Betts injury update: Dodgers star leaves game with back issue

injuries.details: Sprained right ankle on Sept 25, 2024; missed last 3 regular-season games. Played through the injury during the entire postseason, including the World Series where he hit a legendary walk-off grand slam in Game 1. Underwent surgery in early December 2024 — debridement and removal of loose bodies from the right ankle. Surgeons also found torn ligaments, but those had healed enough in the four weeks prior that they did not need surgical repair. Freeman called it 'quite a nasty injury.' Was limited entering Spring Training 2025; cleared to hit but not to run at the start. Expected to be ready for the Tokyo Series opening games as a hitter. Has been recovering well and feeling good.

sources.title: Freddie Freeman status for 2025 Opening Day
Freddie Freeman provides positive update regarding ankle injury
